summary: Leave fullDocument change stream option unset by default
description:
This ticket was split from DRIVERS-2231, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

Note: the {{Watch::FULL_DOCUMENT_DEFAULT}} constant will need to remain in place for BC, but can eventually be removed in the next major version (PHPLIB-818).

summary: findAndModify commands always include new and upsert fields
description:
With respect to the {{returnDocument}} option (mapped to {{new}} in outgoing commands) for findAndModify operations, the CRUD spec states:

bq. This option is sent only if the caller explicitly provides a value. The default is to not send a value.

Similar guidance is provided for the {{upsert}} option.

PHPLIB defaults both fields to {{false}} (along with {{remove}}), and always sends them in outgoing commands. We should either consider relaxing the behavior for {{new}} and {{upsert}}, or amending the CRUD spec to permit this behavior.

FWIW, the server's own defaults for these options is {{false}} and I think it'd unlikely that would ever change (based on previous discussions on {{update}}'s defaults).

summary: Disable client persistence when creating clients in test runners
description:
The unified test runner currently injects a unique connection string option into each client it creates in order to ensure libmongoc clients are not re-used (see: [{{MongoDB\Tests\UnifiedSpecTests\Context::createClient()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.12.0/tests/UnifiedSpecTests/Context.php#L297-L300]). This is needed for {{txnNumber}} assertions, since spec tests expect those to start from {{1}} (not the case if a persistent client is re-used). There is a TODO item to remove that in favor of the {{disableClientPersistence}} option from PHPC-1645. Doing so would likely conserve memory usage since clients would not accumulate throughout the test suite.

A similar change can also be made in the legacy transaction spec test runner, which added a random "p" URI option (see: [{{MongoDB\Tests\SpecTests\fromTransactions()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.12.0/tests/SpecTests/Context.php#L242-L245]).

summary: Unified test runner does not evaluate $$exists within $$unsetOrMatches
description: [{{$$unsetOrMatches}}|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/unified-test-format/unified-test-format.rst#unsetormatches] accepts _anything_ as its argument, and should recursively match an existing value in the actual document against that argument. While POC-ing test changes for DRIVERS-1713, I realized that PHPLIB's Matches constraint doesn't actually handle the case where {{$exists}} appears directly _within_ {{$$unsetOrMatches}}. This is due to [{{Matches::assertMatchesOperator()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.12.0/tests/UnifiedSpecTests/Constraint/Matches.php#L239] not having a case for {{$exists}}, which is presently only handled during document iteration.

summary: Always use count command for estimatedDocumentCount
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2228}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Allow extra fields in results of legacy test runner find operations
description:
A failure was observed in the [fle2-BypassQueryAnalysis.yml|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/client-side-encryption/tests/legacy/fle2-BypassQueryAnalysis.yml#L34] test due to an additional field appearing in the actual result from a {{find}} operation.

The CSFLE legacy test format references the Transactions spec, which in turn references the CRUD spec, where [Extra Fields in Actual Documents|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/crud/tests#extra-fields-in-actual-documents] should apply. It looks like this was never the case, as most all legacy test runner operations use {{ASSERT_SAME_DOCUMENTS}} instead of {{ASSERT_DOCUMENTS_MATCH}}.

Since existing spec tests are passing and this pertains to the legacy test runner, this ticket need only revise the assertion logic for {{find}} operations at this time (in [{{getResultAssertionTypeForCollection}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.12.0/tests/SpecTests/Operation.php#L833]).

summary: Improved change stream event visibility for C2C Replication
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-1975}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: FunctionalTestCase::getModuleInfo may fail to match some phpinfo() values
description:
{{FunctionalTestCase::getModuleInfo()}} was introduced in [5d48a1e|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/5d48a1ed3e3700fb4b53d623977244c461ad699c#diff-93b2fb11cb160b931a627169b6f5cb9c5d12b940e5a7ff1aec827f39abf1fc11R287] for PHPLIB-820. The regex pattern used to match {{phpinfo()}} values will fail to match values that include non-word characters (word characters only include letters, numbers, and underscores) . This can be problematic for values that contain version strings, among other things.

I came across this while implementing PHPC-2103, since the regex pattern originated in PHPC's test suite.

summary: Remove obsolete handling of "options" arg for insertMany
Test with consistent versions of crypt_shared and server
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2355}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Add type definitions to method arguments
description: Argument types should be added where they were not previously added. Note that we cannot make these changes until we've dropped support for PHP 7.1 and earlier, as those versions will see a method signature violation, as shown in [this example|https://3v4l.org/LGNYb].

summary: Add return type definitions to methods
description: In 2.0, we can add return types to all methods that have a single return type (as union types are only available starting on PHP 8). Other methods will retain their return type as documented in PHPDoc blocks.

summary: Support static analysis via Psalm
description:
{panel:title=Epic Summary|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
*Summary*
Support static analysis via Psalm

*Motivation*
Static analysis helps uncover typing issues in PHP applications at build time, despite it being a dynamically typed language. This helps reduce type bugs that occur when assumption about return or argument types no longer hold true.


h4. Cast of Characters
Engineering Lead: Andreas Braun
Document Author: Andreas Braun
Product Owner: Rachelle Palmer
Program Manager: Alexander Golin
Stakeholders: 

h4. Documentation
[Scope Document|some.url]
[Technical Design Document|some.url]
{panel}

summary: Ensure writeConcernError is reported for findAndModify
description:
The [FindAndModify operation|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.8.0/src/Operation/FindAndModify.php#L264)] does not inspect the command result for a {{writeConcernError}} field, in contrast to [what is done in libmongoc|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.17.4/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-collection.c#L3388].

It's possible that this check still happens via libmongoc's RW command runner and [_mongoc_cmd_check_ok_no_wce|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.17.4/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-rpc.c#L1178], but we should confirm that is the case.

summary: Add FAQ section to documentation
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2023}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Add examples for common use cases
description: These examples can take inspiration from the examples contained in the C driver: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/tree/master/src/libmongoc/examples

summary: Add connection examples to documentation
description: The TS Product Report mentions that the PHP driver documentation does not have examples how to connect to servers. A dedicated documentation on how to connect to various topologies and where to get more detailed documentation on connection options.

summary: Client constructor signature should be consistent with Manager
description:
With [1b29a6d|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/1b29a6d71e5511ea46b596461442ca94069bdf42] for PHPLIB-599, the prototype of the Client constructor now differs from Manager in that only the Manager constructor allows null values for each parameter.

Note: Manager specifies {{check_null=true}} for {{Z_PARAM_ARRAY_EX}} when parsing the options arrays, so {{null}} values _are_ supported internally. In the Client constructor, we'll likely need to replace explicit values with empty arrays since we tend to inject options (at least for driver options).

We should consider changing this for consistency. It's quite possible that this could break existing applications that pass {{null}} to the Client constructor when they only want to specify an options array.

I only encountered this issue with respect to the Client constructor, but it's possible there are some other edge cases from the recent type changes that I previously overlooked. I suggest we keep any eye out for those when writing the documentation in PHPLIB-601, as there may be other things we wish to change before release.

summary: DocumentsMatchConstraint $$type operator does not support arrays of multiple types
description:
As discussed in the [CSFLE test README|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/client-side-encryption/tests/README.rst], the {{$$type}} operator should support an array of multiple types, like the server's [$type|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/operator/query/type/] operator.

This was likely overlooked because the only spec test(s) that use this syntax are currently skipped (pending PHPC-1760).

summary: Support "number" alias for $$type spec test operators
description: I recently discovered that PHPLIB never implemented support for the "number" alias in its implementation of the {{$$type}} operator for unified spec tests. DRIVERS-2467 will eventually add regression tests, but we can address this oversight in the IsBsonType constraint today.

summary: Relax acceptance of Int64 for "long" in $$type spec test operators
description:
This came up in the [code review|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/pull/991#discussion_r991754727] for PHPLIB-1001.

Both DocumentMatchConstraint (legacy test runner) and IsBsonType (unified test runner) only allow Int64 objects for "long" on a 32-bit platform (i.e. {{PHP_INT_SIZE}} is 4). There is no need to be this strict, and it could actually lead to an assertion failure since PHPC only instantiates Int64 when necessary. A 64-bit BSON integer with a value in the range of a signed 32-bit integer would decode as an integer primitive.

We can easily change this to just allow "long" to match both Int64 or an integer on any platform.

summary: CSFLE example does not explain how to share data key ids
description:
CSFLE examples in PHP lib create and use data keys inside the same php file, so we can store key ids in variables. However, I a real world usage keys are created in a different script, and then referenced in the app either by {{keyAltName}}, or key ids should be serialized and then deserialized.
It seems that the examples should include one or both of those use cases.

summary: Example code to assist with debugging
description:
PHPLIB can include example code that users can copy into their application to quickly assist with debugging. For example:

 * Class to collect and log commands
 * Class to visualize/log SDAM events (would depend on PHPC-1150), similar to the [sdam_viz|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.6/test/tools/sdam_viz] tool for Node

summary: Create tutorial demonstrating BSON handling of enum types
description: This should cover the design of PHPC-2083 and demonstrate several ways to handle enums in modeled document classes.

summary: Obtain AWS credentials for CSFLE in the same way as for MONGODB-AWS
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2280}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Remove getNonce command usage for 6.2+
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2490}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Propagate Original Error for Write Errors Labeled NoWritesPerformed
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2327}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: InvalidArgumentException::invalidType() should ensure $expectedType is not an empty array
description: [b2cd286|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/b2cd2866c64690603896b788cd56e45ff4cc69a4] for PHPLIB-511 added support for passing an array of strings as the {{$expectedType}} parameter for {{InvalidArgumentException::invalidType()}}. The {{switch}} statement that builds the exception message doesn't gracefully handle an array of zero elements. That should either be handled as an error or an empty string.

summary: Improving Time-Series Scalability
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2546}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Remove usages of xtrace in shell scripts
Add relevant type union syntax to documentation method signatures
description:
Per some feedback in PHPLIB-1055, it would be more helpful for users to include type unions in doc method signatures (e.g. [MongoDB\Database::command()|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/php-library/current/reference/method/MongoDBDatabase-command/] instead of just the parameter table that follows.

This was likely never considered for PHPLIB-599 since we could not utilize PHP 8.0's type unions when implementing other typing improvements in PHPLIB-600; however, there's no technical reason we can't start documenting this, especially since those type requires are already enforced in various places (e.g. operation constructors).

summary: Send readConcern but not writeConcern to explain commands
description:
{{FindAndModify::createCommandDocument()}} does not include the {{writeConcern}} option, as is done for some other commands (e.g. {{Update::getCommandDocument()}}). This only affects the Explainable API (PHPLIB-156), so it's not a critical omission.

While addressing this ticket, we should examine other {{getCommandDocument()}} methods and confirm that they set all command options that may be relevant for the [{{explain}} command|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/explain].

summary: Drivers should not create the ECC collection in v2 of queryable encryption
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2524}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Deprecate currentOp/collStats commands by 7.0
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2232}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Replace operations may inadvertently execute pipeline updates
description:
PHPLIB already implements [update and replacement validation|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/crud.rst#update-vs-replace-validation], and additionally allows pipelines for update arguments.

Replace operations should not allow pipelines, but this is inconsistent. [ReplaceOne|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.15.0/src/Operation/ReplaceOne.php#L93] prohibits pipelines but [FindOneAndReplace|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.15.0/src/Operation/FindOneAndReplace.php#L113] and [BulkWrite replaceOne|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.15.0/src/Operation/BulkWrite.php#L195] do not (likely an oversight in PHPLIB-418). This potentially allows replace operations to execute an update.

Additionally, the {{is_pipeline()}} utility function has historically returned {{false}} for empty pipelines. Technically, empty pipelines are valid NOPs for {{update}} and {{findAndModify}} commands; however, they are rejected by libmongoc for updates because empty arrays and documents have identical {{bson_t}} representations (for BC, libmongoc considers such a {{bson_t}} as an empty replacement document). Comprehensive validation for update and replace operations may require us to consider empty arrays as pipelines, so {{is_pipeline()}} will need to be enhanced. This is also relevant to PHPLIB-881, which will start using the function to validate aggregation pipelines (empty arrays are completely valid there).

CRUD spec tests generally do not test invalid types for operation arguments. Since PHP arrays can express both a document or pipeline array, PHPLIB must be sensitive to replacement documents that resemble pipelines.

h3. Notable edge cases

MongoDB 5.0+ relaxed restrictions on dots and dollars in field names. This is not problematic for replacement documents since those require special syntax (see: [Document replacing updates|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/dot-dollar-considerations/#document-replacing-updates]).

CDRIVER-4658 discusses an edge case where a replacement document might be serialized as an update pipeline. This is due to internal logic in libmongoc's code for assembling an {{update}} command, whereby {{bson_t}} structs resembling a pipeline (i.e. numeric keys and document with a dollar-prefixed key) are encoded as a BSON array instead of a document. It's very unlikely for a user to run into that, but it'd be prudent to add test coverage for it so long as libmongoc maintains this behavior.

summary: Leverage array_key_first in functions
Utilize array_is_list() where we currently detect packed arrays
description:
We can utilize [array_is_list()|https://www.php.net/array_is_list] in various places throughout PHPLIB where we're currently validating arrays. This is likely not an exhaustive list, but for example:

 * {{MongoDB\is_pipeline()}} (used by Update and FindOneAndUpdate)
 * Aggregate validation of {{$pipeline}} parameter
 * BulkWrite validation of {{$operations}} parameter

Note that we'll need to add a dependency for {{symfony/polyfill-php81}} from the [symfony/polyfill|https://github.com/symfony/polyfill] package.

summary: GridFS\Bucket::$disableMD5 not used
description:
[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/pull/1079/files#r1205730523]
{quote}This looks like a bug.

The option was introduced in [f169616|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/f169616c11574e2ea59d2891e5b2d38a7c942fa8] for PHPLIB-319. The [GridFS|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#configurable-gridfsbucket-class] spec notes that it's relevant so long as a driver still supports MD5 hashes, which PHPLIB does.

It looks like {{openUploadStream()}} never inherits the Bucket's default value like it does for {{{}chunkSizeBytes{}}}. I believe all of the existing spec tests only set the option when invoking the operation, so the Bucket inheritance is never tested.
{quote}

summary: Fix CachingIterator::count() on empty Cursor
Ignore writeConcern option for read-only aggregations
description:
When constructing options for {{Server::executeReadWriteCommand}}, a {{writeConcern}} option is ignored if the pipeline does not contain a write stage (e.g. {{$out}}, {{$merge}}). It may make more sense to either prohibit specifying a {{writeConcern}} if the pipeline does not write or ignore it earlier (e.g. unset it in the constructor).

Either change would require changes to some tests within CollectionFunctionalTest, which currently use a non-writing pipeline with a {{writeConcern}} option.

summary: Add documentation for mongocryptd and crypt shared library
description: Client-Side Encryption section does not include any information about mongocryptd and shared crypt library. Even though this is not PHP per se, it would be helpful to provide some information about the required setup for FLE. Otherwise a customer may not be able to even execute given examples.

summary: Support the Azure VM-assigned Managed Identity for Automatic KMS Credentials
description:
This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2411}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description. 

Note: since this ticket likely only requires PHPLIB tests, any documentation changes will pertain to PHPC.

summary: Add support for GCP attached service accounts when using GCP KMS

summary: Use separate namespaces for examples
description:
Hello!

I believe I may have found a small issue when using mongodb/mongodb with
"appstract/laravel-opcache": "^4.0".
{{Appstract\Opcache\Http\Controllers\OpcacheController@compile results in:}}
Symfony\Component\ErrorHandler\Error\FatalError: Cannot redeclare MongoDB\Examples\toJSON() (previously declared in /var/www/html/vendor/mongodb/mongodb/examples/command_logger.php:22)
#0 /vendor/mongodb/mongodb/examples/with_transaction.php(19): null
 
and this

*Symfony\Component\ErrorHandler\Error\FatalError:* Cannot redeclare MongoDB\Examples\toJSON() (previously declared in /var/www/html/vendor/mongodb/mongodb/examples/command_logger.php:22)

summary: Run timeSeries collection creation options tests on MongoDB 6.3
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2720}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Respect useMultipleMongoses:true for non-serverless, load-balanced topologies in legacy spec test runners
description:
When PHPLIB-757 was originally implemented, the legacy test runners were not updated to respect {{useMultipleMongoses: true}} when running on a non-serverless, load-balanced topology. As a result, legacy spec tests run on LB environments always use {{MONGODB_SINGLE_MONGOS_LB_URI}}.

At a glance, this affects the retryable-writes, transactions, and transactions-convenient-api spec tests.

This was discovered while investigating PHPLIB-1228.

summary: Prohibit PackedArray objects where a document type is expected
Consider transaction read preference for server selection
description:
To be fully spec compliant if transactions allow read preferences other than primary, server selection needs to consider the read preference from an active transaction. This currently falls back to a primary read preference but eventually will have to be adapted.

This should _not_ be done for write operations, per existing spec tests.

summary: targetedFailPoint operation in legacy spec test runner should assert that session is pinned
description:
The legacy spec test runner does not assert that a session is actually pinned when executing the [targetedFailPoint|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/transactions/tests/README.rst#targetedfailpoint] operation. This resulted in flaky test failures because the fail point would be configured on the primary server by default.

This was discovered while implementing PHPLIB-1228.

Note that the unified test runner does not have this problem, as its {{targetedFailPoint}} operation [does assert|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/1.16.1/tests/UnifiedSpecTests/Operation.php#L886].

summary: Test FaaS (AWS Lambda) Behavior Per Driver
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2384}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.
